config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena justru hak individu adalah batas agar kekuasaan tidak semena-mena. Dalam praktiknya, alasan "demi masyarakat" sering dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan, dan saya melihat itu berbahaya. Yang lebih tepat adalah mencari keseimbangan, bukan memberi prioritas mutlak pada satu sisi.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically come before the individual, because in the United States we have seen how easily “the public good” can be used to excuse government overreach. Individual rights are not a luxury; they are the guardrails that keep the majority from trampling minorities and unpopular people. At the same time, rights are not absolute in every situation, since public safety and basic order sometimes require limits. So for me the right answer is balance with strong protection for individual liberty, not a blanket rule that society always wins.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Sebagai orang Indonesia, saya justru melihat bahaya ketika “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai terlalu mudah untuk membenarkan pembatasan, apalagi kalau yang dirugikan adalah kelompok kecil atau orang yang tidak punya suara. Tapi saya juga tidak setuju dengan individualisme yang mengabaikan gotong royong, karena dalam kehidupan sosial kita memang ada tanggung jawab bersama. Jadi menurut saya, yang benar bukan memberi prioritas mutlak pada masyarakat, melainkan menempatkan hak individu sebagai batas yang jelas, lalu kepentingan publik hanya boleh dibatasi secara sangat terukur dan adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take automatic priority over the individual, because in the United States that kind of rule has too often become a blank check for government power. I am not defending extreme individualism where no one owes anything to anyone else, but I do believe individual rights have to come first as the default, especially for speech, conscience, and due process. Public interests matter, yet they should justify limits only when they are narrow, necessary, and clearly accountable, not as a vague excuse to override people who are unpopular or inconvenient. To me, a healthy society is one that protects the individual precisely because that is how it stays fair to everyone, including the majority.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi masyarakat” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk menekan orang kecil atau kelompok yang tidak punya kuasa. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi kalau hak individu tidak dijadikan batas yang tegas, negara dan mayoritas bisa semena-mena atas nama kebaikan bersama. Saya juga tidak setuju dengan individualisme ekstrem, karena budaya gotong royong itu nyata dan perlu, tetapi gotong royong tidak boleh berubah menjadi pembenaran untuk mengorbankan hak dasar. Jadi menurut saya, masyarakat dan individu harus seimbang, dengan hak individu tetap dilindungi sebagai pagar utama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over individual rights, because in the United States that idea has too often been used to justify overreach by governments, majorities, or institutions. I am not arguing for selfishness or extreme individualism, but I do think rights like free speech, due process, and conscience should be the default limit on what society can demand. Public interests matter, yet they should only override individual rights in narrow, clearly justified, and accountable cases. A society is healthier when it protects the person first, because that is what prevents “the common good” from becoming a cover for abuse.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
